channel.title: Tech on The Daily Thrum
channel.description: Recent content in Tech on The Daily Thrum

item.title: Job-seeking Candidates Must Traverse 40-metre Corridor While Being Attacked by Masked Martial Artists Armed With Nunchaku In Tech Company's Final Stage of Hiring Process
item.description:
A leading Australian technology company has finally revealed that the mysterious “final cultural and technical assessment” in its hiring process is, in fact, a 40-metre office corridor lined with masked martial artists who attack candidates with nunchaku.

The company, workflow optimisation platform Tyrus Synergy, confirmed the practice after several senior software engineers already tried to go through it and became hospitalised.

“It’s important to assess candidates holistically,” said Tyrus Synergy Head of People Experience, Marissa Klent, standing beside a bloodied whiteboard reading Agile Mindset = Survive. “Anyone can talk about distributed systems in an interview. But can they explain idempotency while evading hits in the ribs by a man in a balaclava? That’s what separates a senior engineer from someone who merely has twenty years of commercial experience.”

item.title: Software developer’s daily stand-up update was “same shit as yesterday”
item.description:
A senior software developer has thrown a medium-sized Agile delivery function into crisis after giving a daily stand-up update so clear, accurate and emotionally honest that several managers were forced to pretend they had not heard it.

Ryan Hollis, 52, delivered the update at 9:46am during what the company officially describes as a “collaborative delivery sync” and what employees privately describe as “the morning hostage roll call”.

When asked what he worked on yesterday, Hollis replied, “same shit as yesterday.”
